csr_facts.text: enum logic [2:0] {Idle, WriteSecond, ReadSecond, WriteResp, ReadResp} state_d, state_q;
assign key    = debug_mode_i ? 256'b0 : {key0[0], key0[1], key0[2], key0[3], key0[4], key0[5], key0[6], key0[7]};
assign ikey_hash = {ikey_hash_bytes[0], ikey_hash_bytes[1], ikey_hash_bytes[2], ikey_hash_bytes[3], ikey_hash_bytes[4], ikey_hash_bytes[5], ikey_hash_bytes[6], ikey_hash_bytes[7]};

assign okey_hash = debug_mode_i ? 256'b0 : {okey_hash_bytes[0], okey_hash_bytes[1], okey_hash_bytes[2], okey_hash_bytes[3], okey_hash_bytes[4], okey_hash_bytes[5], okey_hash_bytes[6], okey_hash_bytes[7]};
assign reglk_ctrl_o = {reglk_mem[5], reglk_mem[4], reglk_mem[3], reglk_mem[2], reglk_mem[1], reglk_mem[0]};

asset_candidates.name: HMAC Key Register
asset_candidates.description: HMAC key value used for cryptographic operations

asset_candidates.name: Access Control Matrix Output
asset_candidates.description: Controls access permission for different peripherals per privilege level

subject_candidates.name: Debug Transport Module (SimDTM)
subject_candidates.description: Simulation debug transport module, can issue debug requests

subject_candidates.name: DMI JTAG Interface
subject_candidates.description: JTAG-DMI bridge for debug command execution

subject_candidates.name: CPU Bus Master
subject_candidates.description: CPU issuing AXI read/write requests via bus interfaces (e.g., buf_*_noc2_data_i)

subject_candidates.name: CPU Privilege Level Input
subject_candidates.description: Privilege level of the current CPU transaction

subject_candidates.name: Test Mode Input
subject_candidates.description: Test mode enable that may bypass security checks

operation_candidates.name: AXI Read
operation_candidates.description: Bus read transaction to peripheral or memory

operation_candidates.name: AXI Write
operation_candidates.description: Bus write transaction to peripheral register
operation_candidates.evidence: buf_*_noc2_data_i with we flag, e.g., we_flag_0

operation_candidates.name: Debug Register Access (DMI)
operation_candidates.description: Debug master read/write via DMI to system bus

operation_candidates.name: Fuse Read
operation_candidates.description: Assertion of fuse request to read fuse data

operation_candidates.name: JTAG Unlock
operation_candidates.description: Setting jtag_unlock to enable debug access

operation_candidates.name: Register Lock Override
operation_candidates.description: Writing to reglk_ctrl to lock/unlock peripheral registers

operation_candidates.name: Access Policy Change
operation_candidates.description: Programming acc_ctrl to alter permission matrix

operation_candidates.name: HMAC Key Load
operation_candidates.description: Loading key material into HMAC key registers

guard_candidates.name: Boot ROM Privilege Gate
guard_candidates.description: Boot ROM access requires appropriate privilege level set in acc_ctrl_c matrix

guard_candidates.name: HMAC Access Control Gate
guard_candidates.description: HMAC wrapper uses acct_ctrl to gate bus transactions

guard_candidates.name: HMAC Register Lock Guard
guard_candidates.description: Lock signal prevents writes to HMAC registers when set

guard_candidates.name: PKT Wrapper Lock Guard
guard_candidates.description: Lock signal protects PKT (key management) registers

guard_candidates.name: REGLK Wrapper JTAG Unlock Bypass
guard_candidates.description: jtag_unlock may bypass reglk lock protection for debugging

guard_candidates.name: Debug Mode Authorization
guard_candidates.evidence: debug_mode_i in hmac_wrapper and other wrappers
guard_candidates.description: Debug mode may allow access regardless of lock/permission status

guard_candidates.name: Test Mode Override
guard_candidates.description: Test mode may bypass normal access controls entirely

state_lifecycle_facts.impact: Controls whether debug/JTAG can override lock protections

state_lifecycle_facts.set_condition: Software write to lock register when lock not active
state_lifecycle_facts.impact: Protects peripheral registers from unauthorized writes
Defines privilege requirements for peripheral access

state_lifecycle_facts.set_condition: Write when reglk_ctrl not active and acct_ctrl permits
state_lifecycle_facts.impact: Confidential key material must not be read back or exposed

state_lifecycle_facts.set_condition: fuse_req asserted, fuse data appears on bus

path_candidates.description: Debug interface can access HMAC registers via DMI bus master, potentially reading/writing keys even if lock set if debug mode authorization missing or jtag_unlock active
Access to boot ROM is gated by privilege level via acc_ctrl. If gate misconfigured, low privilege may read sensitive code.
Test mode may globally disable all access checks, allowing unrestricted read/write to assets
DMA may bypass CPU privilege checks and directly read/write protected areas
Debug path could assert fuse_req and observe fuse values, leaking secret keys stored in fuses
Debug master could reprogram access control matrix or lock states, lowering security permanently

summary: Identified protected assets including HMAC keys, fuse data, JTAG hash, access control state, and lock bits. Subjects include debug/JTAG interfaces, CPU at varying privilege, DMA, and test mode. Operations involved are bus reads/writes, debug access, fuse reads, and lock/unlock. Guards observed include privilege-based gating (acc_ctrl_c), register lock bits (reglk_ctrl), debug mode authorization, and test mode override. Several potential paths were identified where debug or test interfaces might bypass these guards to access or modify sensitive assets.
